[100000-9] Health informatics pioneer and the father of LOINC -- **Component:** Health informatics pioneer and the father of LOINC **Property:** Hx **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nar  **Short name:** Health Info Pioneer+Father of LOINC  **Definition:** This term honors the long-standing contributions of Dr. Clement J. McDonald, the founder of LOINC and health informatics pioneer. Dr. McDonald's dedication, innovation, and hands-on contributions helped develop and continually improve medical informatics and health data standards.
[100001-7] Health informatics pioneer and cofounder of LOINC -- **Component:** Health informatics pioneer and cofounder of LOINC **Property:** Hx **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nar  **Short name:** Health Info Pioneer+Cofound LOINC  **Definition:** This term honors the extraordinary and impactful contributions of Dr. Stanley M. Huff to health data standards, including being one of the original co-creators of LOINC and serving as the Chair of the LOINC Clinical Committee since its inception. Dr. Huff is a renowned expert in representation of medical information in database systems and use of standards for the exchange of data between medical computer systems.

[100017-3] Perioperative nursing data set outcomes panel [PNDS] -- **Component:** Perioperative nursing data set outcomes panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** PNDS  **Definition:** The Perioperative Nursing Data Set (PNDS) outcomes developed by the Association of periOperative Registered Nurses (AORN) are used to assess currently five domains in perioperative care, including safety, physiologic responses, patient and family behavioral responses for knowledge/psychosocial, patient and family behavioral responses for rights/ethics/competency, and health system. The PNDS comprises of data elements and definitions and is used by nurses and clinicians involved in the field of perioperative care. The set of outcomes has evolved over time to address emerging issues and changes in practice.[PMID: 10429784][PMID: 21193085]

[100030-6] Axis I: Temporal aspects score [Mainz Pain Staging System] -- **Component:** Axis I: Temporal aspects score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Mainz Pain Staging System  **Definition:** The Axis I: temporal aspects of pain score is determined by three items including: patterns of occurrence, pain duration, and change of pain intensity. Individual items are scored from 1-3. Items are summed and converted to an axis stage between 3-9 and 3=I, 4-6=II, and 7-9=III, with higher values associated with greater pain chronicity. [PMID: 12799906, PMID: 20093984]
[100031-4] Axis II: Spatial aspects score [Mainz Pain Staging System] -- **Component:** Axis II: Spatial aspects score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Mainz Pain Staging System  **Definition:** The Axis II: spatial aspects of pain score is determined by one item: pain extent. The individual item is scored from 1-3. Items are summed and converted to an axis stage between 1-3 and 1=I, 2=II, and 3=III, with higher values associated with greater pain chronicity. [PMID: 12799906, PMID: 20093984]

[100032-2] Axis III: Drug taking behavior score [Mainz Pain Staging System] -- **Component:** Axis III: Drug taking behavior score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Mainz Pain Staging System  **Definition:** The Axis III: drug taking behavior score is determined by two items including: drug use and number of drug withdrawal treatments. Individual items are scored from 1-3. Items are summed and converted to an axis stage between 2-6 and 2=I, 3-4=II, and 5-6=III, with higher values associated with greater pain chronicity. [PMID: 12799906, PMID: 20093984]
[100033-0] Axis IV: Utilization of the health care system score [Mainz Pain Staging System] -- **Component:** Axis IV: Utilization of the health care system score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Mainz Pain Staging System  **Definition:** The Axis IV: utilization of health care system score is determined by four items including: change of personal physician, pain related hospitalizations, pain related operations, and pain related rehabilitation. Individual items are scored from 1-3. Items are summed and converted to an axis stage between 4-12 and 4=I, 5-8=II, and 9-12=III, with higher values associated with greater pain chronicity. [PMID: 12799906, PMID: 20093984]

[100197-3] Exercise activity and pain severity panel -- **Component:** Exercise activity & pain severity panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** -
[10019-8] R' wave amplitude in lead V4 -- **Component:** R' wave amplitude.lead V4 **Property:** Elpot **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Heart **Scale:** Qn **Method:** EKG  **Short name:** R' wave Amp L-V4
[100198-1] Pre-op objective knee indicators score [Knee Society Score] -- **Component:** Pre-op objective knee indicators score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Knee Society Score  **Definition:** The objective knee score is calculated using alignment, instability, and joint motion. Alignment is calculated on a weight-bearing AP radiograph with a 25-point maximum. Instability is calculated in full extension and 90 degrees with a 25-point maximum. Joint Motion is calculated with 1 point for each 5 degrees of joint motion and there are deductions for flexion contracture and extension lag. [https://www.kneesociety.org/the-knee-society-score]
[100199-9] Pre-op symptoms score [Knee Society Score] -- **Component:** Pre-op symptoms score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Knee Society Score  **Definition:** The symptoms score has a 25-point maximum and is the sum of the scores for pain with level walking (10 point maxium), pain with stairs or inclines (10 point maximum), and knee feel "normal" to you? (5 point maximum).
[100200-5] Pre-op patient expectations score [Knee Society Score] -- **Component:** Pre-op patient expectations score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Knee Society Score  **Definition:** The patient expectation score has a 15-point maximum and is the sum of the scores for expectations to relieve knee pain (5 point maximum), ability to perform activities of daily living (5 point maximum), and ability to perform leisure, recreational, or sports activities (5 point maximum). [https://www.kneesociety.org/the-knee-society-score]
[100201-3] Pre-op functional activities score [Knee Society Score] -- **Component:** Pre-op functional activities score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Knee Society Score  **Definition:** The functional activities score is calculated using walking and standing (30 point maximum), standard activities (30 point maximum), and advanced activities (25 point maximum).   The walking and standing score has a 30-point maximum and is the sum of the scores for the ability to walk without aids (0 points) with deductions (-10 maximum) for type of aids used, duration of standing before sitting due to knee discomfort (15 point maximum), and duration of walking before sitting due to knee discomfort (15 point maximum).   The standard activities score has a 30-point maximum and is the sum of the scores for how bothersome the knee is when walking on uneven surface (5 point maximum), turning or pivoting (5 point maximum), climbing up or down stairs (5 point maximum), getting up from low seat without armrests (5 point maximum), getting into or out of a car (5 point maximum), and moving laterally (5 point maximum).  The advanced activities score has a 25-point maximum and is the sum of the scores for how bothersome the knee is when climbing ladder or step stool (5 point maximum), carrying a bag for one block (5 point maximum), squatting (5 point maximum), kneeling (5 point maximum), and running (5 point maximum). [https://www.kneesociety.org/the-knee-society-score]
[100202-1] Pre-op discretionary knee activities score [Knee Society Score] -- **Component:** Pre-op discretionary knee activities score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Knee Society Score  **Definition:** The discretionary knee activities score has a 15-point maximum and is calculated using three activities patients select. The score is the sum of each activity and how bothersome the knee is during the activity (5 point maximum each). [https://www.kneesociety.org/the-knee-society-score]
[100203-9] Knee Society Score post-op panel [Knee Society Score] -- **Component:** Knee Society Score post-op panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** Knee Society Score  **Definition:** The new Knee Society Scoring System (KSS) integrates physician and patient derived components during the pre-operative period and post-operative period. The new KSS examines five components including patient demographics, objective knee indicators, patient expectations, patient satisfaction, functional knee indicators. [https://www.kneesociety.org/the-knee-society-score]

[100207-0] Post-op total score [Knee Society Score] -- **Component:** Post-op total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Knee Society Score  **Definition:** The total score has a 100-point maximum and is the sum of the sections.
[100208-8] Post-op objective knee indicators score [Knee Society Score] -- **Component:** Post-op objective knee indicators score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Knee Society Score  **Definition:** The objective knee score is calculated using alignment, instability, and joint motion. Alignment is calculated on a weight-bearing AP radiograph with a 25-point maximum. Instability is calculated in full extension and 90 degrees with a 25-point maximum. Joint Motion is calculated with 1 point for each 5 degrees of joint motion and there are deductions for flexion contracture and extension lag. [https://www.kneesociety.org/the-knee-society-score]
[100209-6] Post-op symptoms score [Knee Society Score] -- **Component:** Post-op symptoms score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Knee Society Score  **Definition:** The symptoms score has a 25-point maximum and is the sum of the scores for pain with level walking (10 point maxium), pain with stairs or inclines (10 point maximum), and knee feel "normal" to you? (5 point maximum). [https://www.kneesociety.org/the-knee-society-score]
[100210-4] Post-op patient expectations score [Knee Society Score] -- **Component:** Post-op patient expectations score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Knee Society Score  **Definition:** The patient expectation score has a 15-point maximum and is the sum of the scores for the accuracy of pre-surgery expectations to relieve knee pain (5 point maximum), expectations to perform activities of daily living (5 point maximum), and expectations to perform leisure, recreational, or sports activities (5 point maximum). [https://www.kneesociety.org/the-knee-society-score]
[100211-2] Post-op functional activities score [Knee Society Score] -- **Component:** Post-op functional activities score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Knee Society Score  **Definition:** The functional activities score is calculated using walking and standing (30 point maximum), standard activities (30 point maximum), and advanced activities (25 point maximum).   The walking and standing score has a 30-point maximum and is the sum of the scores for the ability to walk without aids (0 points) with deductions (-10 maximum) for type of aids used, duration of standing before sitting due to knee discomfort (15 point maximum), and duration of walking before sitting due to knee discomfort (15 point maximum).   The standard activities score has a 30-point maximum and is the sum of the scores for how bothersome the knee is when walking on uneven surface (5 point maximum), turning or pivoting (5 point maximum), climbing up or down stairs (5 point maximum), getting up from low seat without armrests (5 point maximum), getting into or out of a car (5 point maximum), and moving laterally (5 point maximum).  The advanced activities score has a 25-point maximum and is the sum of the scores for how bothersome the knee is when climbing ladder or step stool (5 point maximum), carrying a bag for one block (5 point maximum), squatting (5 point maximum), kneeling (5 point maximum), and running (5 point maximum). [https://www.kneesociety.org/the-knee-society-score]
[100212-0] Post-op discretionary knee activities score [Knee Society Score] -- **Component:** Post-op discretionary knee activities score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Knee Society Score  **Definition:** The discretionary knee activities score has a 15-point maximum and is calculated using three activities patients select. The score is the sum of each activity and how bothersome the knee is during the activity (5 point maximum each). [https://www.kneesociety.org/the-knee-society-score]

[100224-5] Cardiac left ventricular segmental wall motion by echo panel -- **Component:** Segmental wall motion panel **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Heart.ventricle.left **Scale:** - **Method:** US  **Short name:** Cardiac LV SWM Pnl LV US  **Definition:** Left ventricular (LV) segmental wall motion (SWM) is used to evaluate LV function. Each segment corresponds to a specific perfusion territory and is evaluated independently. A 17-segment model is usually used for LV function in general; however, the 17th segment (the cardiac apex or apical cap) is typically not included in wall motion studies. Wall motion can be described as normal or hyperkinetic, hypokinetic, dyskinetic, and akinetic. [PMID: 25559473] This LOINC panel contains terms to report the SWM for the 16 segments as described above, as well as additional segments that may be included depending on local practices.
[100225-2] Bone density quantitative ultrasound study -- **Component:** Bone density quantitative ultrasound study **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Doc  **Short name:** Bone density QUS  **Definition:** Quantitative ultrasound (QUS) is an ultrasound technique that is used for the evaluation of osteoporosis. One transducer is placed on either side of a bone, where one transmits ultrasound waves and the other receives them. QUS parameters include broadband ultrasound attenuation (BUA), which is expressed in decibel per megahertz, and speed of sound (SOS), which is expressed in meters per second. SOS is correlated with bone mineralization. BUA and SOS can be further used to derive variables including stiffness index, QUS index, amplitude-dependent speed of sound (AD-SoS), and bone transmission time (BTT). Results are reported as a percentage comparing patient values to reference values based on young adults or weight-matched references, depending on the manufacturer. [PMID: 29541626]
[100226-0] Small bowel capsule endoscopy study Document -- **Component:** Small bowel capsule endoscopy study **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Doc  **Short name:** Sm bowel capsule endo study Doc  **Definition:** Capsule endoscopy is a non-invasive procedure used to evaluate various types of gastrointestinal pathology. It involves the patient swallowing a capsule containing cameras that capture images of the gastrointestinal tract. Small bowel capsule endoscopy is used to evaluate for a variety of conditions including Crohn's disease, celiac disease, and suspected small bowel bleeding. [PMID: 33111600]
[100227-8] Colon capsule endoscopy study Document -- **Component:** Colon capsule endoscopy study **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Doc  **Short name:** Colon capsule endo study Doc  **Definition:** Capsule endoscopy is a non-invasive procedure used to evaluate various types of gastrointestinal pathology. It involves the patient swallowing a capsule containing cameras that capture images of the gastrointestinal tract. Colon capsule endoscopy is primarily used to evaluate for colon neoplasia in different situations, such as when colonoscopy is not feasible or was not able to be completed, or as a screening for low- or moderate-risk populations. [100342-5] Volatile Organic Compounds associated with SARS-CoV-2 infection [Presence] in Exhaled gas by Gas chromatography-mass spectrometry -- **Component:** Volatile Organic Compounds associated with SARS-CoV-2 infection **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Exhl gas **Scale:** Ord **Method:** GC-MS  **Short name:** VOCs (volatiles) SARS-CoV-2 ExG Ql GC-MS  **Definition:** This is a rapid breathalyzer test from InspectIR Systems LLC, for use at the point-of-care.  It can detect five volatile organic compounds that are associated with SARS-CoV-2 infection. The VOCs are in the ketone and aldehyde families, but more detailed information is not available.  Due to potential issues with cross reactivity, positive tests are resulted as "Presumptive positive" and must be confirmed with a highly sensitive FDA Authorized molecular assay.

[100709-5] Hepatocellular carcinoma risk [Score] by GAAD -- **Component:** Hepatocellular carcinoma risk **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** GAAD  **Short name:** Hep Ca Risk Scre Patient GAAD  **Definition:** The GAAD score is used to predict the probability of developing early-stage hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC). It is calculated based on gender, age, alpha-fetoprotein (AFP), and des-gamma-carboxy prothrombin (DCP, also called PIVKA-II). In contrast, the GALAD score [LOINC: 96450-2] includes AFP-L3. The risk-factor score ranges from 0-10 where a higher GAAD scores correlate with increased risk of HCC.

[100842-4] Nonalcoholic steatohepatitis score in Serum or Plasma Calculated by FAST -- **Component:** Nonalcoholic steatohepatitis score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated.FAST  **Short name:** NASH score SerPl Calculated.FAST  **Definition:** Fast  is a score used by healthcare professionals to aid in the identification of patients with suspicion of NAFLD as being at risk for active fibrotic NASH (NASH+NAS≥4+F≥2). Fast is computed from liver stiffness by US.transient elastography, liver ultrasound attenuation by transient elastography, and AST blood parameter measurement. More information and the formula can be found at https://www.echosens.com/products/fast.
[100843-2] Liver fibrosis score in Serum or Plasma by Calculated.Agile3+ -- **Component:** Liver fibrosis score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser/Plas **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Calculated.Agile3+  **Short name:** Liver fibr score SerPl Agile3+  **Definition:** Agile is a set of scores estimating the degree of liver fibrosis in patients with suspicion of nonalcoholic liver diseases. Agile 3+ is based on Liver Stiffness measurement and a variety of parameters (e.g. lab parameters AST, ALT, Platelets and Age, Gender, Diabetes status) to assess the probably of advanced fibrosis. More information and the formula can be found at https://www.echosens.com/products/agile-3/.

[100916-6] User satisfaction score [R-Outcomes] -- **Component:** User satisfaction score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** R-Outcomes  **Definition:** Each level is indicated using a traffic-light metaphor with written labels, color, position and emojis. For individuals, each item response level is scored on a 0-3 ordinal scale, with strongly agree = 3, agree = 2, neutral = 1, and disagree = 0. A summary score is calculated by adding the item scores for each item and giving a scale from 0 to 12. A summary score is not calculated if any item scores are missing. For cohorts, mean scores are shown on a 0 to 100 scale. Item scores are multiplied by 100 and divided by 3; summary scores are multiplied by 100 and divided by 12. Mean scores are usually rounded to the nearest whole number [PMID: 32198234].

A summary score is calculated by adding the item scores for each item and giving a scale from 0 to 12. A summary score is not calculated if any item scores are missing. For cohorts, mean scores are shown on a 0 to 100 scale. Item scores are multiplied by 100 and divided by 3; summary scores are multiplied by 100 and divided by 12. Mean scores are usually rounded to the nearest whole number [PMID: 32198234].

[101114-7] NCSBN ID -- **Component:** National Council of State Boards of Nursing ID **Property:** ID **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Provider **Scale:** Nom  **Short name:** NCSBN ID  **Definition:** The National Council of State Boards of Nursing (NCSBN) ID is a unique, eight-digit number assigned to every nurse upon their first licensure with the board of nursing. NCSBN ID numbers are available through the NURSYS system. [https://www.ncsbn.org/nursys.htm]

A higher score indicates that identification is possible at the species level, versus an intermediate score that allows identification at the genus level. Low scores do not allow identification of the organism from the unknown sample. The specific scores at which genus- and species-level identification is possible vary by microorganism. [101662-5] Abnormal Prion Protein [Presence] in Cerebral spinal fluid by RT-QuIC -- **Component:** Prion protein.abnormal **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** CSF **Scale:** Ord **Method:** RT-QuIC  **Short name:** Abn Prion Prot CSF Ql RT-QuIC  **Definition:** Detection of pathologic prions in cerebrospinal fluid (CSF). The test uses recombinant prion protein (PrP) as a reagent substrate and patient CSF in a real-time quaking-induced conversion (RT-QuIC) assay. If pathologic prions are present in the patient's CSF, they propagate misfolding of the reagent PrP to form amyloid, which is detected using a fluorescent indicator. RT-QuIC has been adopted in clinical practice due to the sensitivity, specificity, and use of CSF (not a brain biopsy) for testing. The reported sensitivity of RT-QuIC in detecting CJD is >90%, and the specificity approaches 100%.

[101697-1] Post traumatic stress disorder checklist - civilian version DSM-5 [PCL-5] -- **Component:** Post traumatic stress disorder checklist - civilian version DSM-5 **Property:** - **Time aspect:** 1Mo **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** PCL-5  **Definition:** The PCL-5 is a 20-item self-report measure of the DSM-5 symptoms of PTSD. The wording of PCL-5 items reflects both changes to existing symptoms and the addition of new symptoms in DSM-5.   The PCL has a variety of purposes including, screening individuals for PTSD, diagnosing PTSD and monitoring symptom change during and after treatment. The PCL-C (civilian) asks about symptoms in relation to "stressful experiences." The PCL-C is useful because it can be used with any population. The symptoms endorsed may not be specific to just one event, which can be helpful when assessing survivors who have symptoms due to multiple events. Typically, it is optimal to assess traumatic event exposure to ensure that a respondent has experienced at least one Criterion A event.

[101951-2] NIPS assessment (interpretation) -- **Component:** NIPS Assessment **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** NIPS inter  **Definition:** Interpretation of the total score:  Score 0 = no pain. [101967-8] Neonatal infant pain scale panel (NIPS) -- **Component:** Neonatal infant pain scale panel (NIPS) **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** NIPS  **Short name:** NIPS panel  **Definition:** The Neonatal Infant Pain Scale (NIPS) is a behavioral pain scale that was developed by the Children's Hospital of Eastern Ontario.  It can be utilized with both full-term and pre-term infants.  CHEO recommends use in neonates/infants less than 2 months of age, but the scale can be used up to 1 year of age.  The tool was adapted from the CHEOPS scale (used for children between one and seven years) and is based on the behaviors that nurses have described as being indicative of infant pain or distress.  It is composed of six indicators.  The infant should be observed for one minute in order to fully assess each indicator.

[101994-2] Foundation pain index Document by based on lab data -- **Component:** Foundation pain index **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Based on lab data  **Short name:** FPI Patient Doc from lab data  **Definition:** Foundation Pain Index (FPI) is a multi-analyte assay with algorithmic analysis (MAAA) that improves patient outcomes by identifying underlying, treatable causes of pain. FPI assists providers in identifying underlying determinants of pain while simultaneously indicating personalized, non-opioid pain therapies. The pain index is reported as a singular numerical score on a scale of 1-100 which is further classified into categories representing the likelihood that detected biochemical abnormalities are indeed a determinant or cause of pain.

[102036-1] Abnormal Prion Protein [Interpretation] in Cerebral spinal fluid Document by RT-QuIC -- **Component:** Prion protein.abnormal **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** CSF **Scale:** Doc **Method:** RT-QuIC  **Short name:** Abn Prion Prot CSF Doc RT-QuIC-Imp  **Definition:** Transmissible spongiform encephalopathies (TSEs or prion diseases) are a rare group of invariably fatal neurodegenerative disorders that affect humans and other mammals. TSEs are protein misfolding diseases that involve the accumulation of an abnormally aggregated form of the normal host prion protein (PrP). They are unique among protein misfolding disorders in that they are transmissible and have different strains of infectious agents that are associated with unique phenotypes in vivo.  https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19157856/#:~:text=Abstract,host%20prion%20protein%20(PrP).

[103775-3] Consolability -- **Component:** Consolability **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Consolability  **Definition:** 1. No need to console is characterized by a sense of well-being. The person appears content.  2. Distracted or reassured by voice or touch is characterized by a disruption in the behavior when the person is  spoken to or touched. The behavior stops during the period of interaction, with no indication that the person is at all  distressed.  3. Unable to console, distract, or reassure is characterized by the inability to soothe the person or stop a behavior with  words or actions. No amount of comforting, verbal or physical, will alleviate the behavior.
[103776-1] Breathing -- **Component:** Breathing **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Breathing
[103777-9] Negative vocalization -- **Component:** Negative vocalization **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Neg vocal  **Definition:** 1. None is characterized by speech or vocalization that has a neutral or pleasant quality.  2. Occasional moan or groan is characterized by mournful or murmuring sounds, wails, or laments. Groaning is characterized by louder than usual inarticulate involuntary sounds, often abruptly beginning and ending.  3. Low level speech with a negative or disapproving quality is characterized by muttering, mumbling, whining, grumbling, or swearing in a low volume with a complaining, sarcastic, or caustic tone.  4. Repeated troubled calling out is characterized by phrases or words being used over and over in a tone that suggests anxiety, uneasiness, or distress.  5. Loud moaning or groaning is characterized by mournful or murmuring sounds, wails, or laments in much louder  than usual volume. Loud groaning is characterized by louder than usual inarticulate involuntary sounds, often abruptly beginning and ending.  6. Crying is characterized by an utterance of emotion accompanied by tears. There may be sobbing or quiet weeping.
[103778-7] Facial expression -- **Component:** Facial expression **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Facial expression
[103779-5] Body language -- **Component:** Body language **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Body language  **Definition:** 1. Relaxed is characterized by a calm, restful, mellow appearance. The person seems to be taking it easy.  2. Tense is characterized by a strained, apprehensive, or worried appearance. The jaw may be clenched. (Exclude any contractures.)  3. Distressed pacing is characterized by activity that seems unsettled. There may be a fearful, worried, or disturbed element present. The rate may be faster or slower.  4. Fidgeting is characterized by restless movement. Squirming about or wiggling in the chair may occur. The person might be hitching a chair across the room. Repetitive touching, tugging, or rubbing body parts can also be observed.  5. Rigid is characterized by stiffening of the body. The arms and/or legs are tight and inflexible. The trunk may appear  straight and unyielding. (Exclude any contractures.)  6. Fists clenched is characterized by tightly closed hands. They may be opened and closed repeatedly or held tightly shut.  7. Knees pulled up is characterized by flexing the legs and drawing the knees up toward the chest. An overall troubled appearance. (Exclude any contractures.)  8. Pulling or pushing away is characterized by resistiveness upon approach or to care. The person is trying to escape by yanking or wrenching him- or herself free or shoving you away.  9. Striking out is characterized by hitting, kicking, grabbing, punching, biting, or other form of personal assault.

[103844-7] HBA1 and HBA2 gene deletion in Amniotic fluid or Chorionic villus sample by Molecular genetics method -- **Component:** HBA1 & HBA2 gene deletion **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Amnio fld/CVS **Scale:** Doc **Method:** Molgen  **Short name:** HBA1 + HBA2 Del Amn/CVS  **Definition:** Alpha-globin is an essential component of the hemoglobin tetramer, starting from the early stages of embryonic development. Deletion mutations involving  one or both of the two alpha-globin genes (alpha1 and alpha2, located on chromosome 16p13) lead to reduced production of alpha-globin chains, and are  the major cause of alpha-thalassemia. Severity of the disease is dependent on the total copy number of functional alpha-globin genes remaining.  This assay detects the seven most common deletions (-alpha3.7, -alpha4.2, -alpha20.5, --SEA, --MED, -FIL, and --THAI) found in patients with alpha-thalassemia. This assay is performed by allele-specific PCR amplification of deletion mutation fragments, followed by agarose gel electrophoresis of the amplification products.

[104003-9] Medical Expenditure Panel Survey (MEPS) Health Insurance (HX) Section [MEPS.HX] -- **Component:** Medical Expenditure Panel Survey (MEPS) Health Insurance (HX) Section **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** MEPS.HX  **Definition:** The Medical Expenditure Panel Survey (MEPS) is a set of large-scale surveys of families and individuals, their medical providers, and employers across the United States. MEPS is the most complete source of data on the cost and use of health care and health insurance coverage. The Health Insurance section (HX) collects information about private health insurance obtained through an employer, direct purchase private insurance plans, and public health insurance programs. It identifies the household members covered by health insurance, type of plan, name of each plan, nature of coverage under each plan, duration of coverage, and who pays various costs for the policy premiums. It also identifies the household members not covered by health insurance.

[104010-4] 16-item Transportation Security Index [TSI-16] -- **Component:** 16-item Transportation Security Index **Property:** - **Time aspect:** 30D **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** TSI-16  **Definition:** The 16-item Transportation Security Index captures the lived experiences of people experiencing transportation insecurity, and it has been validated using a nationally representative survey. The index can be used to assign respondents a continuous score representing their level of transportation insecurity or to categorize respondents as experiencing no insecurity or insecurity that is marginal, low, moderate, or high. [104644-0] Cobalt IgE Ab [Presence] in Serum by Radioallergosorbent test (RAST) -- **Component:** Cobalt Ab.IgE **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Ser **Scale:** SemiQn **Method:** RAST  **Short name:** Cobalt IgE RAST  **Definition:** This test is a specific immunological test that measures the level of Immunoglobulin E (IgE) antibodies against cobalt in a person's blood serum. IgE antibodies are produced by the immune system in response to exposure to certain allergens or substances, and their presence indicates an allergic reaction.  This test is used to help diagnose occupational asthma or other respiratory allergic conditions caused by exposure to cobalt, which is a low molecular weight agent commonly found in various industrial and manufacturing settings.[PMID 7939614]

[106410-4] Candida spp. 1 DNA [Cycle Threshold #] in Positive blood culture by NAA with probe detection -- **Component:** Candida albicans+tropicalis+dubliniensis+famata+guilliermondii+kefyr DNA **Property:** ThreshNum **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld.pos growth **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Probe.amp.tar  **Short name:** Cand spp. 1 Ct Bld + Qn NAA+prb  **Definition:** This term represents a test for detecting and quantifying the DNA of multiple Candida species (Candida albicans, tropicalis, dubliniensis, famata, guilliermondii, and kefyr) using probe amplification. The results are reported as a threshold number, indicating the presence and quantity of the Candida DNA in the sample. This method is particularly useful for diagnosing infections caused by these Candida species in immunocompromised patients and hospital settings, as referenced in [PMID: 25183855].
[106411-2] Candida spp. 2 DNA [Cycle Threshold #] in Positive blood culture by NAA with probe detection -- **Component:** Candida glabrata+krusei+parapsilosis+lusitanieae DNA **Property:** ThreshNum **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld.pos growth **Scale:** Qn **Method:** Probe.amp.tar  **Short name:** Candida spp. 2 DNA Ct Bld +Qn NAA+prb  **Definition:** This term represents a test for detecting and quantifying the DNA of multiple Candida species (Candida glabrata, krusei, parapsilosis, and lusitaniae) using probe amplification. [106415-3] Hester Davis scale total score -- **Component:** Hester Davis scale total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Hester Davis scale total score  **Definition:** The Hester Davis Scale measures risk for anticipated physiologic falls using the factors of age, date of last known fall, mobility, medications, mental status, toileting needs, volume/electrolyte status, communication/sensory, and behavior, with multiple options per risk category.  The category scores are summed to calculate the level of risk for falls.  The scale has been validated for sensitivity and specificity in scoring fall risk.
[106416-1] Hester Davis fall risk scale -- **Component:** Hester Davis fall risk scale **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** -  **Short name:** Hester Davis fall risk scale  **Definition:** The HDS© fall risk assessment tool is used to identify acute care adult patients at high risk for falling, and also identifies why the patient is at higher risk so that individualized interventions can be included in the plan of care.  The scale is composed of 9 risk factors.  The presence of a risk factor is scored as 1 (absence is scored zero).  The HDS psychometric evidence supports high content validity, very high construct validity, and reliability.  A total score of one or more indicates high risk for falling.    The Hester Davis Scale measures risk for anticipated physiologic falls using the factors of age, date of last known fall, mobility, medications, mental status, toileting needs, volume/electrolyte status, communication/sensory, and behavior, with multiple options per risk category.  The category scores are summed to calculate the level of risk for falls.  The scale has been validated for sensitivity and specificity in scoring fall risk.

[106418-7] Three-Item Loneliness scale [Three-Item Loneliness Scale] -- **Component:** Three-Item Loneliness scale **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** Three-Item Loneliness Scale  **Definition:** The Three-Item Loneliness Scale was developed specifically for use on a telephone survey. This is a scale used to assess feelings of loneliness or social isolation. It was developed from the Revised UCLA (R-UCLA) Loneliness Scale. Each question is rated on a 3-point scale: 1 = Hardly Ever; 2 = Some of the Time; 3 = Often. All items are summed to give a total score.

[106422-9] 6-item Transportation Security Index total score [TSI-6] -- **Component:** Total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** 30D **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** TSI-6  **Definition:** To score the TSI-6, look at responses to the main questionnaire, assign each "never" response a score of 0, each "sometimes" a score of 1, and each "often" a score of "2."    For those interested in using the TSI-6 as a continuous variable, sum the scores to arrive at the candidate's continuous transportation insecurity score.    Add together the individual scores for all six items. The total score should be between 0 and 12.
[106423-7] 6-item Transportation Security Index [TSI-6] -- **Component:** 6-item Transportation Security Index **Property:** - **Time aspect:** 30D **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** TSI-6  **Definition:** The 6-item Transportation Security Index is an abbreviated version of the 16-item Transportation Security Index. It captures the lived experiences of people experiencing transportation insecurity, and it has been validated using a nationally representative survey. The index can be used to assign respondents a continuous score representing their level of transportation insecurity or to categorize respondents as experiencing no insecurity or insecurity that is marginal, low, moderate, or high.

[106428-6] 16-item Transportation Security Index total score [TSI-16] -- **Component:** Total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** TSI-16  **Definition:** Looking at responses to the main questionnaire, assign each "never" response a score of 0, each "sometimes" response a score of 1, and each "often" response a score of 2. Sum the scores to arrive at the respondent's continuous transportation insecurity score. Add together the individual scores for all sixteen items. The total score should be between 0 and 32.

[106442-7] Cumulative dose [Dosage] Lifetime -- **Component:** Cumulative dose **Property:** Dosage **Time aspect:** Lifetime **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Cumulative dose Lifetime  **Definition:** Cumulative dose in the Childhood Cancer Survivorship Passport represents any other treatment (not included in the chemotherapy, surgery or radiotherapy sections) was adminstered to the survivor for a specific drug. [106622-4] Cesarean status -- **Component:** Cesarean status **Property:** Type **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Cesarean status  **Definition:** This term cesarean status refers to the level of urgency for performing a caesarean section, categorizing the procedure based on the immediacy of the need for intervention. [106875-8] Perceived stress scale-10 [PSS-10] -- **Component:** Perceived stress scale-10 **Property:** - **Time aspect:** RptPeriod **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** PSS-10  **Definition:** The PSS is a self-reported measure designed to deal with the degree to which situations in an individual's life are appraised as stressful. It was originally developed as a 14-item scale that assessed the perception of stressful experiences over the previous month using a five-point Likert scale. Later, the authors reported that the 10-item version (PSS-10) showed stronger psychometric characteristics in comparison to the 14-item scale (Cohen & Williamson, 1988).
[106876-6] Hemoglobin-haptoglobin complex [Mass/volume] in Stool -- **Component:** Hemoglobin-haptoglobin complex **Property:** MCnc **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Stool **Scale:** Qn  **Short name:** Hp-Hb complex Stl-mCnc
[10688-0] Microscopic observation [Identifier] in Hair by KOH preparation -- **Component:** Observation **Property:** Prid **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Hair **Scale:** Nom **Method:** KOH preparation  **Short name:** KOH Prep Hair  **Definition:** A KOH Preparation involves treating a specimen with KOH, a strong base that can make fungi and yeast more visible by partial dissolution of tissue cells, keratin, and other material. Fungal cells are resistant due to the high content of cellular chitin. KOH prep is generally a test for fungi, most often candida yeast, but others too, such as tinea capitis and other skin or nail fungal infections, even blastomycosis, etc. However, the KOH prep is also used to diagnose infections with mites (Demodex folliculorum in rosacea-like lesions and Sarcoptes scabiei in hyperkeratotic or crusted scabies) and sometimes even nematodes (in blue neck syndrome). [PMID:16766846]
[106881-6] PROMIS Item Bank v1.0 - Emotional Distress - Anxiety - Short Form 8a -- **Component:** PROMIS Item Bank v1.0 - Emotional Distress - Anxiety - Short Form 8a **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** PROMIS  **Definition:** The PROMIS Item Bank v1.0 - Emotional Distress - Anxiety - Short Form 8a is a collection of eight items drawn from PROMIS item bank - emotional distress - anxiety - version 1.0 (61922-1).    PROMIS® (Patient Reported Outcomes Measurement Information System) instruments measure concepts such as pain, fatigue, physical function, depression, anxiety and social function by asking patient's questions about how they feel and what they are able to do. PROMIS® instruments may be used in clinical studies to provide clinicians and researchers with information on the effectiveness of therapy and development of treatment plans. The instruments are also used by patients and physicians to improve communication and manage chronic disease.

[106939-2] Amyloid probability score in Plasma by Calculation -- **Component:** Amyloid plaques probability score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Plas **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Calculation  **Short name:** Amyloid prob score Plas Calc  **Definition:** The term repersents The PrecivityAD2™ test. This test is an analytically and clinically validated blood test designed to assist healthcare providers in diagnosing Alzheimer's disease (AD) in patients with mild cognitive impairment or dementia. It quantifies specific plasma amyloid beta and tau peptides using liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry (LC-MS/MS) to calculate the Aβ42/40 and p-tau217/np-tau217 ratios. These ratios are combined in a proprietary algorithm to produce the Amyloid Probability Score 2 (APS2), a numerical value from 0-100 that indicates the likelihood of brain amyloid plaques. By providing a non-invasive alternative to PET scans, the PrecivityAD2™ test enhances diagnostic accuracy while mitigating the effects of confounding factors.

PROMIS® (Patient Reported Outcomes Measurement Information System) instruments measure concepts such as pain, fatigue, physical function, depression, Depression and social function by asking patient's questions about how they feel and what they are able to do. [107617-3] Abbreviated Child and Adult Food Security Scale [ACAFSS] -- **Component:** Abbreviated Child and Adult Food Security Scale **Property:** - **Time aspect:** RptPeriod **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** ACAFSS  **Definition:** The Abbreviated Child and Adult Food Security Scale (ACAFSS) is a highly sensitive, specific, and valid scale for detecting severity levels of Food Insecurity (FI) among adults and children in household. The ACAFSS is recommended as a stand-alone scale or a follow-up scale after households with children screen positive for FI risk. The ACAFSS is also recommended for planning interventions and evaluating their effects not only on the binary categories of food security and FI, but also on changes in levels of severity, especially when rapid decision making is crucial.

[107619-9] eHealth Literacy Scale [eHEALS] -- **Component:** eHealth Literacy Scale **Property:** - **Time aspect:** RptPeriod **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** eHEALS  **Definition:** The eHealth Literacy Scale (eHEALS) is an 8-item measure developed to measure consumers' combined knowledge, comfort, and perceived skills at finding, evaluating, and applying electronic health information to health problems. The objective of the study was to psychometrically evaluate the properties of the eHEALS within a population context.
